note: Shared list locking the risky-pattern heuristic across runtimes. `risky` are patterns QRID_riskyPattern / CheckCharacter::riskyPattern must reject (config error, no compile); `safe` must pass through. The gate has two stages. Stage one rejects nested quantifiers (bounded and unbounded) AND any repetition of a group that can match ambiguously — alternation, optionals, or inner quantifiers — with inner groups collapsed layer by layer so nesting cannot hide the shape. Stage two rejects the POLYNOMIAL class stage one misses: two or more unbounded quantifiers (*, +, {n,}) over overlapping character classes with no mandatory separator between them (.*.*, [0-9]*[0-9]*, [A-Z]+[A-Z0-9]+) — these freeze a browser's backtracking engine even though PCRE2 auto-possessifies them. It deliberately over-rejects (a repeated alternation is refused even with disjoint branches; a collapsed group is treated as overlapping everything). It is still a heuristic, not a proof: a BOUNDED backtracker like A{1,40}A{1,40}A{1,40}9 passes (its work is bounded by the pattern, not the input length), which is why the server keeps the match-time PCRE-error guard as a backstop. `risky_js.cjs` additionally times every `safe` pattern against adversarial input, so "safe" is measured, not assumed.
